name: Stack of Nodes
summary: Traverse the list once to push node IDs onto a stack, then pop them to rebuild the links in reverse order.
visualizationIntent: init: create the linked-list nodes and original next arrows, loop: move current through the original list while pushing each node onto a stack, check: detect whether the first popped node should become the new head or should be attached after the current tail, update: rewrite the tail's next arrow to point to the popped node and break the popped node's old next arrow, move: advance current or tail to the next node being processed, return: traverse the rebuilt list and show the reversed values as the final result.

lineExplanations.3: Start with an empty list that will hold the values of the reversed linked list.
lineExplanations.4: Copy the input values into node storage so each value can be treated like a linked-list node.
lineExplanations.5: Count how many nodes are in the linked list.
lineExplanations.6: Create one next-link slot per node, initially pointing to -1, which means no next node.
lineExplanations.7: Start at the first node index while building the original forward links.
lineExplanations.8: Continue building original links while there is another node after index i.
lineExplanations.9: Set node i to point to the next node, i + 1.
lineExplanations.10: Move i forward so the next original link can be created.
lineExplanations.11: Create an empty stack that will store node IDs in the order they are visited.
lineExplanations.12: Begin traversal at node 0, the head of a non-empty list.
lineExplanations.13: Check whether the list is empty.
lineExplanations.14: For an empty list, use -1 so the traversal loop immediately stops.
lineExplanations.15: Start with no highlighted changed links.
lineExplanations.16: Traverse the original list until current moves past the end.
lineExplanations.17: Push the current node ID onto the stack.
lineExplanations.18: Follow the current node's original next pointer.
lineExplanations.19: The new head is unknown until the first node is popped from the stack.
lineExplanations.20: The tail of the rebuilt reversed list is also unknown at first.
lineExplanations.21: Initialize node so the visualization has a real pointer variable before popping begins.
lineExplanations.22: Keep rebuilding while the stack still contains nodes.
lineExplanations.23: Pop the most recently visited node, which should come next in reversed order.
lineExplanations.24: Check whether this popped node is the first node of the reversed list.
lineExplanations.25: Make the first popped node the head of the reversed list.
lineExplanations.26: The first popped node is also the current tail.
lineExplanations.28: Mark the old tail because its next link is about to be rewritten.
lineExplanations.29: Connect the current tail to the newly popped node.
lineExplanations.30: Move the tail pointer to the node just attached.
lineExplanations.31: Mark the new tail because its old next link must be cleared.
lineExplanations.32: Set the new tail's next pointer to -1 so it properly ends the partial reversed list.
lineExplanations.33: Clear link-change highlighting before reading out the final reversed list.
lineExplanations.34: Start the final readout traversal from the new head.
lineExplanations.35: Walk through the rebuilt linked list until the end is reached.
lineExplanations.36: Read the value stored in the current node.
lineExplanations.37: Append that value to the returned result list.
lineExplanations.38: Move to the next node in the reversed linked list.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the class LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def reverseList(self, head): defines the method; head is the input list of linked-list values.
syntaxExplanations.3: result = [] creates an empty Python list.
syntaxExplanations.4: head[:] makes a shallow copy of the input list.
syntaxExplanations.5: len(nodes) returns the number of items in nodes.
syntaxExplanations.6: [-1] * n creates a list containing n copies of -1.
syntaxExplanations.8: while i < n - 1: repeats the indented block while the condition is true.
syntaxExplanations.9: links[i] = i + 1 writes a value into links at index i.
syntaxExplanations.10: i += 1 is shorthand for i = i + 1.
syntaxExplanations.11: stack = [] creates an empty list used with append and pop as a stack.
syntaxExplanations.12: current = 0 stores the index of the first node.
syntaxExplanations.13: if n == 0: runs the next indented line only when n is zero.
syntaxExplanations.14: current = -1 uses -1 as a sentinel meaning no node.
syntaxExplanations.15: changed_links = [] creates an empty list of recently changed link IDs.
syntaxExplanations.16: while current != -1: repeats while current is a valid node ID.
syntaxExplanations.17: stack.append(current) adds current to the end of the list.
syntaxExplanations.18: current = links[current] follows the next pointer stored for current.
syntaxExplanations.19: new_head = -1 initializes new_head to the no-node sentinel.
syntaxExplanations.20: tail = -1 initializes tail to the no-node sentinel.
syntaxExplanations.21: node = -1 initializes node before the first pop.
syntaxExplanations.22: while len(stack) > 0: repeats while the stack is not empty.
syntaxExplanations.23: stack.pop() removes and returns the last item in the list.
syntaxExplanations.24: if new_head == -1: checks whether no head has been chosen yet.
syntaxExplanations.25: new_head = node assigns the current node ID to new_head.
syntaxExplanations.26: tail = node assigns the current node ID to tail.
syntaxExplanations.27: else: starts the branch used when the if condition is false.
syntaxExplanations.28: changed_links = [tail] creates a one-item list containing tail.
syntaxExplanations.29: links[tail] = node rewrites the next pointer of the tail node.
syntaxExplanations.30: tail = node moves the tail variable to node.
syntaxExplanations.31: changed_links = [tail] creates a one-item list containing the new tail.
syntaxExplanations.32: links[tail] = -1 makes the tail point to no node.
syntaxExplanations.33: changed_links = [] clears the list of changed links.
syntaxExplanations.34: current = new_head starts another traversal from the rebuilt head.
syntaxExplanations.35: while current != -1: loops while current is a real node ID.
syntaxExplanations.36: value = nodes[current] reads the value stored at the current node ID.
syntaxExplanations.37: result.append(value) appends value to the result list.
syntaxExplanations.38: current = links[current] follows the next pointer in the rebuilt list.
syntaxExplanations.39: return result sends the final list back to the caller.
